input.prompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

data.content:
## Migrating MSTest 3.8.0 to Microsoft.Testing.Platform

### 1. Add a `Directory.Build.props` (shared across all projects)

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <UseTestingPlatform>true</UseTestingPlatform>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

- **`UseTestingPlatform`** – switches MSTest 3.8+ from VSTest to the new platform.
- **`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`** – keeps `dotnet test` working (it bridges the new runner back to the `dotnet test` CLI).

### 2. Each test `.csproj` – no structural changes needed

MSTest 3.8.0 already bundles the MTP runner; the properties above activate it. Just confirm each project references:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
```

(If you're still using the older split packages `MSTest.TestAdapter` + `MSTest.TestFramework`, consolidate to the unified `MSTest` meta-package.)

### 3. TRX logger

Add the TRX output package to each project (or via `Directory.Packages.props`):

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.*" />
```

### 4. CI command change

```bash
# Before
dotnet test --logger trx

# After
dotnet test --logger trx
```

The command stays the same — `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` ensures `dotnet test` and `--logger trx` continue to work unchanged. The TRX files are emitted via the new extension package instead of the VSTest adapter.

### Summary of changes

| Scope | Change |
|-------|--------|
| `Directory.Build.props` | Add `UseTestingPlatform` + `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` |
| Each `.csproj` | Add `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` package |
| CI command | None (stays `dotnet test --logger trx`) |
